creator: w.schwarzenfeld@utanet.at
text:
Cannot reproduce this error with 10.4 and 11.1 (on amd64):

configure:25310: $? = 0
configure:25317: result: yes
configure:25336: result: ARPACK-NG library found
configure:25360: checking whether the arpack library works
configure:25515: c++ -o conftest -O2 -pipe -DLIBICONV_PLUG -fstack-protector -fno-strict-aliasing  -DLIBICONV_PLUG -I/usr/local/include -DLIBICONV_PLUG  -L/usr/local/lib -Wl,-rpath=/usr/local/lib/gcc6  -L/usr/local/lib/gcc6 -B/usr/local/bin -L/usr/local/lib -fstack-protector conftest.cpp -larpack -llapack -lblas  -lcurses  -lm  -L/usr/local/lib -L/usr/local/lib/gcc6 -L/usr/local/bin -L/usr/local/lib/gcc6/gcc/x86_64-portbld-freebsd11.1/6.4.0 -L/usr/local/lib/gcc6/gcc/x86_64-portbld-freebsd11.1/6.4.0/../../../../../x86_64-portbld-freebsd11.1/lib -L/usr/local/lib/gcc6/gcc/x86_64-portbld-freebsd11.1/6.4.0/../../.. -lcurses -lgfortran -lm -lssp_nonshared -lquadmath >&5
configure:25515: $? = 0
configure:25515: ./conftest
configure:25515: $? = 0
configure:25525: result: 
configure:25536: result: Working ARPACK-NG library found (probably ARPACK-NG or a patched version of ARPACK)



Maybe, this error is overcome by events.
But I had another error see Bug #225628 or Bug #221601 (should updated to version 6.0.0).

Secand question is if this is a maintainer timeout?
